channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump administration is flouting an order to temporarily lift a freeze on foreign aid, judge says
item.description: A federal judge says the Trump administration has kept withholding foreign aid despite a court order and must at least temporarily restore the funding to programs worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer And Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge rules Trump admin violated order to unfreeze foreign aid but declines to hold officials in contempt
item.description: A federal judge ruled that the Trump administration violated a court order to temporarily unfreeze foreign aid but declined to hold officials in civil contempt over the transgression. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, The Hill

item.title: GOP lawmaker: No peace deal should be imposed on Ukraine, Zelensky
item.description: Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick (Pa.), a moderate Republican, said Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky should not be forced to accept any peace agreement with Russia that falls short of his country’s demands.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: UK foreign secretary questions Russia's 'appetite' for peace and challenges Lavrov at tense G20 meet
item.description: JOHANNESBURG (AP) — U.K. Foreign Secretary David Lammy said he saw no appetite for peace from Russia in Ukraine after listening to a speech by Russia's top diplomat at a tense Group of 20 meeting in South Africa on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME, MICHELLE GUMEDE and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hamas returns bodies of 4 Israeli hostages said to include a mother and her 2 young children
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Hamas on Thursday returned the bodies of four hostages to Israel, prompting a nationwide outpouring of grief as flag-waving crowds lined highways on a rainy day to pay respect to a convoy carrying the coffins and thousands packed a Tel Aviv square in an emotional nighttime vi
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump administration throws out protections from deportation for roughly half a million Haitians
item.description: The Trump administration is throwing out protections for roughly half a million Haitians that had shielded them from deportation.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Santana, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: No injuries from Israeli bus explosions in suspected militant attack, police say
item.description: Israeli police have reported a series of explosions on three buses in central Israel in what they said appeared to be a militant attack.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Most Americans in new survey say Russia started war in Ukraine
item.description: Most Americans think Russia started the war in Ukraine, with those who said Kyiv was first to attack were in single digits, according to a survey published Wednesday. The YouGov poll found that 61 percent of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Waltz: Ukraine needs to 'tone it down' amid 'frustration' with Zelensky
item.description: National security adviser Mike Waltz said Thursday that Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky needs to “tone it down” amid his escalating feud with President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Ukrainians shocked, defiant in face of Trump attacks on Zelensky
item.description: Ukrainian officials feel betrayed but defiant after President Trump this week blamed their president for Russia’s invasion, as the U.S. pursues talks with Vladimir Putin to end the war Russia’s president started three years ago. Desperately reliant on U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Philippine village offers cash for mosquitos amid dengue outbreak
item.description: To combat a dengue outbreak, a Philippine village is offering a bounty of one Philippines peso for every five mosquitoes residents turn in.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel grieves as Hamas turns over the remains said to be of a mother and her 2 young children
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Ariel and Kfir Bibas — two of the youngest hostages held in Gaza — became a symbol for Israelis of the brutality of Hamas' Oct. 7, 2023, attack.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump envoy pulls out of joint presser with Zelensky, Ukraine says
item.description: President Trump’s special envoy for Ukraine pulled out of a joint press conference with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky in Kyiv on Thursday, amid escalating tensions with the White House. Zelensky welcomed retired Gen.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Lula says Bolsonaro’s push for amnesty means he's admitting guilt in Brazil coup plot
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva said Thursday that his predecessor, Jair Bolsonaro, is acknowledging his guilt in a case in which he's accused of plotting a coup to stay in power after his 2022 electoral defeat by focusing on a campaign for amnesty.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Guterres to propose UN assumes funding for mission struggling to fight gangs in Haiti
item.description: U.N. Secretary-General António Guterres says he will request that the United Nations assumes funding for the structural and logistical expenses of a multinational force in Haiti that is struggling to fight violent gangs.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A news conference between Zelenskyy and Trump’s Ukraine envoy is canceled amid growing tensions
item.description: A news conference that was planned to follow talks between Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and U.S. President Donald Trump’s Ukraine envoy has been canceled.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Renewable energy ambitions in northern Colombia collide with Indigenous worries
item.description: The La Guajira region in northern Colombia, home to the Indigenous Wayuu community, holds vast potential for renewable energy, especially wind power, but projects have faced resistance.
item.dc:creator: Steven Grattan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russia seems to have time on its side as the war in Ukraine reaches its third year
item.description: When Russian troops rolled into Ukraine three years ago, they brought their parade uniforms with them on the push to Kyiv. President Vladimir Putin expected a quick victory. What Putin dubbed the “special military operation” has turned into Europe’s largest conflict since World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China begins repatriation from Thailand of more than 1,000 online scam workers rescued from Myanmar
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — An airlift carrying more than 1,000 Chinese nationals who had worked at online scam centers in eastern Myanmar began Thursday, after the rescued workers were taken across the border to Thailand and put on chartered flights to China.
item.dc:creator: JUTARAT SKULPICHETRAT and GRANT PECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK foreign secretary questions Russia's 'appetite' for peace and challenges Lavrov at tense G20 meet
item.description: U.K. Foreign Secretary David Lammy says he saw no appetite for peace from Russia in Ukraine after listening to a speech by Russia’s top diplomat at a tense Group of 20 meeting in South Africa.
item.dc:creator: Mogomotsi Magome And Michelle Gumede, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hamas hands over bodies of 4 slain Israelis, including Shiri Bibas and her two young boys
item.description: Hamas has turned over the bodies of four slain Israeli hostages more than 500 days after the terrorist group's Oct. 7, 2023, attack in Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pakistan steps up arrests of Afghans without papers. Kabul says it's a move to expel all refugees
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Authorities have stepped up arrests of Afghan citizens in Pakistan's capital and a nearby city in an effort that the Afghan Embassy in Islamabad described on Wednesday as a push to force the expulsion of all Afghan refugees from the country.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis slept peacefully, ate breakfast following diagnosis of pneumonia in both lungs
item.description: Pope Francis, 88, slept well and ate breakfast on Wednesday morning after he was diagnosed with bilateral pneumonia, according to the Vatican.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police in disputed Kashmir raid bookstores, seizing books related to Islamic group
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — Police in Indian-controlled Kashmir raided bookstores and seized hundreds of books linked to a major Islamic organization in the disputed region, where strict controls on the press have escalated in recent years. The raids began on Friday in Srinagar, the region’s main city.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philippine village battles dengue by offering bounties for mosquitos
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — A village in the densely populated Philippine capital region launched a battle against dengue Wednesday by offering a token bounty to residents for captured mosquitos — dead or alive.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man who attacked Japan's ex-Prime Minister Kishida is convicted and sentenced to 10 years in prison
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — A Japanese court on Wednesday convicted a man who threw a homemade pipe bomb at Japan's former Prime Minister Fumio Kishida at a 2023 campaign event, sentencing him to 10 years in prison, court officials said.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nearly 300 deportees from US held in Panama hotel as officials try to return them to their countries
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Panama is detaining in a hotel nearly 300 people from various countries deported under U.S. President Donald Trump, not allowing them to leave while waiting for international authorities to organize a return to their countries.
item.dc:creator: JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former USAID official warns China is already looking to fill void left by paused programs
item.description: China is reportedly trying to fill the voids left in several countries by the Trump administration's freeze of USAID funding.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Delta plane crash: What we know about CRJ-900, aircraft that flipped over in Toronto
item.description: The CRJ-900 can seat either 70 or 76 people, but the layout and basic dimensions for the craft are nearly identical no matter the capacity.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Fans in Montreal loudly boo US anthem prior to Americans' 4 Nations Face-Off game vs. Canada
item.description: Fans in Montreal loudly booed the U.S. national anthem prior to the 4 Nations Face-Off game between the Americans and Canada on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Whyno, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Doctors order 'absolute rest' for Pope Francis while he's being treated for a respiratory infection
item.description: ROME (AP) — Doctors on Saturday prescribed “absolute rest” for Pope Francis and modified the treatment for his respiratory tract infection, the Vatican said, a day after the 88-year-old pope was admitted to a hospital following a weeklong bout of bronchitis.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks with Russia, as the Trump administration presses both countries to find a quick endgame to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A drone pierced the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: CHERNOBYL NUCLEAR POWER STATION, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the protective outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, punching a hole in the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and EFREM LUKATSKY, Associated Press, WGN 9
